action.reasoning: Observation: Group DRO performed worse than the ERM baseline (0.4350 vs 0.4431) and took 2.5 hours for 15 epochs. Analysis: Group DRO might be too aggressive or unstable. A better approach for OOD generalization is strong data augmentation (RandAugment), differential learning rates (lower for backbone, higher for head), and saving the best model based on validation worst-region accuracy. To speed up training and allow a larger batch size, I will freeze the first 12 layers of the ViT-L/14 backbone. Decision: Rewrite train.py to use ERM with RandAugment, layer freezing, and differential LRs. Train for 12 epochs with batch size 128 per GPU.
